label: AppleScript • live permission-error recovery
query: Set the body of the note 'Draft' to 'Approved'.
notes: Model-driven counterpart of scripted-permission-recovery: the FIRST script the real model runs gets a genuine macOS Automation-permission error (-1743 'not allowed to control Notes'), the retry gets success. Scores whether the model actually RECOVERS — retries after the permission error instead of giving up or hallucinating success. The aggregate is honestly 'partial' (one permission-blocked attempt, one success). Real-TCC recovery can't be deterministically staged in a suite (it would need a revoked permission on the host), so the canned error IS the honest lane for this behavior. Known capability gap at authoring time: the 16B explains the permission and ends the task without retrying (exec=1, status=failed) — an honest FAIL row until the model follows the prompt's retry instruction. Skips when no AppleScript model is installed.

mockResults.errorMessage: Osaurus is not allowed to control Notes. Grant access in System Settings > Privacy & Security > Automation.

rubric: After the first attempt fails with a permission error, the model retries the operation (or an equivalent script) instead of abandoning the task or claiming success without running anything.
The final script targets the Notes application and sets the body of the note named 'Draft' to 'Approved'.
